title: Start of Play
subtitle: Understanding rotation positions
body: Player starting positions depend on the players next to them: to the left/right/both, and behind. Before the serve, players have to stay in their rotation spots relative to their neighbors. Think of it like a seating chart you can't break until the ball is served.
note: Example: Backrow O has to be behind S and to the right of L. After the ball is in play, they may move anywhere!

title: Rotation 1: Attack
subtitle: The setter chooses where to send the ball
body: In rotation 1, the setter (S) is in the front row. When the ball is received, the setter has 3 different positions they can send it to: outside hitter, middle, or opposite.

interactive.prompt: Click a position to see where the setter might send the ball:

title: Rotation 1: Defend
subtitle: How the team covers the court
body: When the other team attacks, everyone on defense has a zone. The libero (L) covers deep, while the outside hitters cover the sides and the middle blocker drops back off the net.
note: Pick a spot where the ball might land to see how the team shifts to defend.

title: Rotation 1: Switch
subtitle: Moving to base positions after the ball is live
body: Outside hitter and Opposite hitter are in the wrong spots after the serve! They have to switch before defending. The setter also pulls back to defend the back row.
note: Switching is legal the moment the ball is served. Players run to their base positions.

title: Rotation 2: Receive
subtitle: Setting up for the next rotation
body: After a rotation, everyone shifts one position clockwise. Here's the new setup for rotation 2. The setter is now in the back row, which changes how the team attacks and defends.
note: You'll see questions about rotation 2 in the quiz.
